title: Today's AI Brief: Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
summary: AI Brief: Boston Children's Hospital uses AI for rare disease diagnoses, Google's AI Mode changes search in the US, and more.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Let's dive into some exciting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Boston Children's Hospital is leveraging OpenAI technology to improve patient care and diagnose rare diseases. This is huge, as we're talking about conditions that can be super tricky to pin down.

Boston Children's Hospital has successfully used OpenAI's AI technology to help diagnose over 40 rare disease cases. What's really cool here is that the AI system is helping to reduce the operational burden on healthcare professionals, allowing them to focus on what matters most - patient care. I mean, who wouldn't want a super-smart sidekick helping you crack tough medical mysteries? It's not just about the numbers, though - it's about the impact on people's lives.

Google's rolling out its AI Mode in the US, changing the way people search online. With AI Mode, users can get more direct and concise answers to their queries, rather than a list of links. It's like having a personal research assistant built right into your search engine. I've been playing around with it, and it's pretty impressive how well it can cut through the noise.

Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.
